title: schmaltzia michauxii
intro: Schmaltzia michauxii, also known as American Storax or Spicewood, is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to the southeastern United States. It is a member of the Anacardiaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The resin of the Schmaltzia michauxii plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The resin can be prepared into herbal preparations, such as tinctures, salves, and infusions, to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
